name: Bridge Creek Fresh Ginger Muffins
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Lemon zest (from 2 lemons with some white pith)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease muffin tins.
3. Cut the unpeeled ginger into large chunks and process in a food processor until it is in tiny pieces (about 1/4 cup). It's better to have too much than too little.
4. Put the ginger and 1/4 cup sugar in a small skillet and cook over medium heat until the sugar has melted and the mixture is hot. This should take only a couple of minutes; do not leave unattended. Remove the ginger mixture from the stove and allow it to cool.
5. In a food processor, put the lemon zest and 3 tablespoons sugar and process until the lemon peel is in small bits. Alternatively, chop the lemon zest and pith by hand and then add the sugar. Add this lemon mixture to the ginger.
6. Beat the butter in a mixing bowl for a second or two, then add the remaining 1/2 cup sugar and beat until smooth. Add the eggs and beat well. Add the buttermilk and mix until blended.
7. Add the flour, salt, and baking soda. Beat until smooth. Add the ginger-lemon mixture and mix well.
8. Spoon the batter into the muffin tins so that each cup is three-quarters full. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes. Serve warm.
